text:
## Database Architecture  
### Primary Database (PostgreSQL)  
**Database:** `firstuk_prod`  
**Schemas:**
- `public.accounts` — Account records (800 records)
- `public.customers` — Customer records (500 records)
- `public.transactions` — Transaction log (2,000+ records/month)
- `public.account_holders` — Joint account holders
- `public.standing_orders` — Recurring transfers
- `public.direct_debits` — Recurring bill payments
- `public.cards` — Debit card records (800 cards)
- `public.compliance_kyc` — KYC verification records
- `public.compliance_aml` — AML screening records
- `public.products` — Product definitions (5 products)  
**Indexes:**
- Customer ID (for lookups)
- Account ID (for queries)
- Transaction date (for history)
- Account status (for reporting)  
**Backup Strategy:**
- Continuous replication to standby database
- Daily encrypted backup to AWS S3
- RPO: 15 minutes
- RTO: 2 hours
name: Chunk 22/39

raw_entities.name: FirstUK Production Database
raw_entities.description: The primary PostgreSQL relational database serving as the central data store for FirstUK Bank's core banking operations. It hosts critical schemas for customer, account, transaction, and compliance data, supporting high-volume operational queries and historical reporting.

raw_entities.name: Standby Database
raw_entities.description: A continuously replicated secondary database instance designed to ensure high availability and support disaster recovery operations. It maintains a synchronized copy of the primary database to minimize service disruption during failover events.

raw_entities.name: Account
raw_entities.description: Represents individual and joint account records stored within the primary database schema. It tracks customer financial positions, balances, and lifecycle states for operational and reporting purposes.

raw_entities.name: Customer
raw_entities.description: Stores core customer identification and profile data required for banking relationships and regulatory compliance. It serves as the foundational party entity linked to accounts, transactions, and cards.

raw_entities.name: Transaction
raw_entities.description: Captures detailed transactional activity logs, processing a volume exceeding 2,000 records per month. It maintains chronological financial event data for historical analysis, reconciliation, and customer reporting.

raw_entities.name: Account Holder
raw_entities.description: Manages joint account holder relationships and ownership structures within the database schema. It defines multi-party access rights and liability allocations for shared financial products.

raw_entities.name: Standing Order
raw_entities.description: Tracks recurring customer transfer instructions and scheduled payment mandates. It stores authorization details, frequency parameters, and execution history for automated fund movements.

raw_entities.name: Direct Debit
raw_entities.description: Records automated bill payment authorizations and recurring debit instructions for customers. It maintains mandate validity, collection schedules, and dispute statuses for utility and service payments.

raw_entities.name: Card
raw_entities.description: Stores debit card issuance details, status, and lifecycle records for the bank's payment portfolio. It tracks cardholder assignments, PIN statuses, and authorization limits.

raw_entities.name: KYC Verification
raw_entities.description: Maintains Know Your Customer verification records and identity documentation for regulatory compliance. It stores customer due diligence results, risk ratings, and document expiry dates.

raw_entities.name: AML Screening
raw_entities.description: Holds Anti-Money Laundering screening results, watchlist checks, and compliance audit data. It records transactional risk assessments and regulatory reporting outcomes for suspicious activity monitoring.

raw_entities.name: Product
raw_entities.description: Defines the bank's core financial offerings, including their structural parameters and feature sets. It serves as the master reference for account types, interest rates, and eligibility criteria.

raw_entities.name: Recovery Point Objective (RPO)
raw_entities.description: Specifies the maximum acceptable data loss window during a disaster recovery event, mandated at 15 minutes. It dictates the required frequency of database replication and backup operations to meet continuity standards.

raw_entities.name: Recovery Time Objective (RTO)
raw_entities.description: Defines the maximum allowable downtime for system restoration following a disruption, set at 2 hours. It establishes the operational performance threshold for failover processes and infrastructure recovery procedures.

key_features: Indexed lookups for Customer ID, Account ID, Transaction date, Account status

regulatory_significance: Mandatory compliance requirement for customer identity verification
Mandatory compliance requirement for transaction and account monitoring

properties.what_it_measures: Maximum acceptable data loss window during a disaster recovery event
properties.context: Defined in primary database backup strategy

properties.what_it_measures: Maximum acceptable downtime before systems must be restored to operational status
properties.context: Defined in primary database backup strategy

relationships.description: Stores account records in the public.accounts schema
Stores customer records in the public.customers schema
Stores transaction logs in the public.transactions schema
Stores joint account holder data in the public.account_holders schema
Stores recurring transfer instructions in the public.standing_orders schema
Stores recurring bill payment data in the public.direct_debits schema
Stores debit card records in the public.cards schema
Stores KYC verification records in the public.compliance_kyc schema
Stores AML screening records in the public.compliance_aml schema
Stores product definitions in the public.products schema
Replicates data continuously to the standby database for disaster recovery and high availability
Tracks data loss tolerance to ensure recovery point does not exceed 15 minutes
Tracks system restoration tolerance to ensure recovery time does not exceed 2 hours
